en.extract:
The ochre-rumped bunting (Emberiza yessoensis), also known as the Japanese reed bunting, is a bird in the family Emberizidae. It is found in Manchuria, Korea and Japan. Its natural habitats are temperate grassland and swamps. It is threatened by habitat loss. In South Korea it is classified as an endangered species.


Taxonomy
The bird family Emberizidae contains 44 seed-eating species confined to the Old World. The ochre-rumped bunting was first described as Schoenicola yessoensis by Robert Swinhoe in 1874. The species epithet, yessoensis, describes the bird as being found on Yesso (Hokkaido). Within its genus, the ochre-rumped bunting is most closely related to the common reed bunting and Pallas's reed bunting, which have sometimes been classified as being in their own genus Schoeniclus.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognised- E. y. continentalis, which breeds in eastern Mongolia, northeast China and Ussuriland and winters in east China and E. y. yessoensis which breeds and winters in Japan and also winters in Korea.


Description
It is 15 cm in length. Typically, it is the richest-coloured of the reed buntings, with the pinkest legs and bill in winter. Adult males have a dark back, chestnut upperparts, and are striped black and buff on the breast and sides. They have a brown nape, and the sides of neck are whitish. Females have a buffy submoustachial and throat, black malar stripes, and a dark brown crown with pale streaking. Juveniles have a pale greyish-brown central crown stripe and a yellowish brown rump. 
The calls of the ochre-rumped bunting can be transcribed as 'sur-swee-ik' and 'tik'.


References

The '''ochre-rumped bunting''' ('''''Emberiza yessoensis'''''), also known as the '''Japanese reed bunting''', is a [[bird]] in the family [[Emberizidae]]. It is found in [[Manchuria]], Korea and Japan. Its natural [[habitat]]s are temperate [[grassland]] and [[swamp]]s.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite journal |last=BirdLife International (BirdLife International) |date=2020-08-06 |title=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species: ''Emberiza yessoensis'' |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22721016/181093139 |journal=IUCN Red List of Threatened Species |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250222191649/https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22721016/181093139 |archive-date=2025-02-22 |access-date=2025-03-12 |url-status=live }}</ref> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].<ref name=":0" /> In [[South Korea]] it is classified as an endangered species.<ref name=":0" />
==Taxonomy==
The bird family [[Emberizidae]] contains 44 seed-eating species confined to the Old World. The ochre-rumped bunting was [[Species description|first described]] as ''Schoenicola yessoensis'' by [[Robert Swinhoe]] in 1874.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=국립생물자원관 한반도의 생물다양성 (NIBR: Details - ''Emberiza yessoensis'') |url=https://species.nibr.go.kr/home/mainHome.do?cont_link=009&subMenu=009002&contCd=009002&pageMode=view&ktsn=120000002233 |access-date=2025-03-12 |website=species.nibr.go.kr}}</ref><ref name=swinhoe>{{cite Q|Q103898639| url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/56942483|volume=4|pages=150-166 [161]|issue=2 |series=3}}</ref> The species epithet, ''yessoensis'', describes the bird as being found on Yesso ([[Hokkaido]]).<ref name="swinhoe" /><ref>{{Cite journal |last=Blakiston, T. |date=1863 |title=Corrections and Additions to Captain BLAKISTON'S Paper "On the Ornithology of Northern Japan" |url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/54848021
|journal=Ibis|volume=IV|issue=16| page=99}}</ref> Within its genus, the ochre-rumped bunting is most closely related to the [[common reed bunting]] and [[Pallas's reed bunting]], which have sometimes been classified as being in their own genus ''Schoeniclus''.

===Subspecies===
Two subspecies are recognised- ''E. y. continentalis'', which breeds in eastern [[Mongolia]], northeast [[China]] and [[Ussuriland]] and winters in east China and ''E. y. yessoensis'' which breeds and winters in [[Japan]] and also winters in [[Korea]].<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Copete |first1=J.L |title=Ochre-rumped Bunting |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/ocrbun1/cur/introduction#subsp |website=Birds of the World |doi=10.2173/bow.ocrbun1.01 |s2cid=216258322 |access-date=8 May 2020|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Description==
It is 15 cm in length. Typically, it is the richest-coloured of the reed buntings, with the pinkest legs and bill in winter. Adult males have a dark back, chestnut upperparts, and are striped black and buff on the breast and sides. They have a brown nape, and the sides of neck are whitish. Females have a buffy submoustachial and throat, black malar stripes, and a dark brown crown with pale streaking. Juveniles have a pale greyish-brown central crown stripe and a yellowish brown rump. 
The calls of the ochre-rumped bunting can be transcribed as 'sur-swee-ik' and 'tik'.

zh.extract:
红颈苇鹀（学名：Emberiza yessoensis）为雀科鹀属的鸟类，俗名黑头。分布于日本、朝鲜半岛、俄罗斯、蒙古以及中国大陆的黑龙江、吉林、曾见于辽宁、河北、山东、江苏、福建等地，多生活于松嫩平原的沿江地带、多在长着各种柳丛、小灌丛和水草的沼泽地和附近生长着各种草丛的地方以及尤喜在草丛等处。该物种的模式产地在?日本。


亚种
红颈苇鹀东北亚种（学名：Emberiza yessoensis continentalis）。分布于俄罗斯、日本以及中国大陆的黑龙江、吉林、河北、山东、江苏、福建等地。该物种的模式产地在江苏南京。
